skill.name: reply
skill.description: Review p11 document comments and replies, then post useful replies to visible threads that ask questions, are unclear, are indecisive, or need input. Use when Claude Code should answer reviewers on a p11 document.
skill.summary: Review p11 document comments and post concise replies where reviewer input is needed.
skill.author: rarexlabs

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography findings at SKILL.md:3 and agents/openai.yaml:3 are false positives; those lines contain human-readable descriptions, not cryptographic code. Static external-command findings are real because the skill instructs assistants to run p11 commands and install or upgrade a global npm CLI. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, but publication should include a command-execution warning.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution and Global Installation
medium_findings.description: The skill directs assistants to run p11 CLI commands and install or upgrade @p11-core/cli globally when needed. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but it can execute external software and post replies to p11 documents, so users should approve the CLI and target before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly call command-line tools and global npm installation. The commands match the documented p11 workflow, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings are not supported by the file contents. SKILL.md:3 is the skill description, and agents/openai.yaml:3 is a short interface description; neither line contains cryptographic code or weak hashing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct line review confirms both reported locations are plain text descriptions. No cryptographic algorithm usage is present at either location.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell command examples for p11 history, p11 comments, p11 reply, command discovery, and npm global installation. These commands are intentional for the workflow but should be treated as external-command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple explicit command invocations and an npm global install instruction are present. The commands are not hidden or obfuscated, which supports a medium rather than high severity rating.

content.value_statement: Review threads often stall when questions, conflicts, or unclear comments are not answered. This skill helps Claude Code inspect visible p11 comment threads and post concise replies where reviewer input is needed.
actual_capabilities: Resolves a p11 target from a provided URL, ID, current chat context, or p11 history.
Checks p11 CLI availability and gives installation guidance for @p11-core/cli.
Fetches p11 document comments and replies for review.
Identifies visible threads that ask questions, need clarification, or contain conflicting replies.
Posts replies to selected comment threads using the p11 reply command.
Summarizes clear decisions that should be handled by a separate revision workflow.
limitations: Requires access to the p11 CLI or permission to install it globally.
Can only act on comments visible through p11 commands and the provided target.
Does not revise document content; it only replies to review threads.
May need user confirmation before posting replies that affect external documents.

use_cases.title: Answer Reviewer Questions
use_cases.description: Review visible p11 comments and reply to questions that block document progress.

use_cases.title: Resolve Unclear Feedback
use_cases.description: Find threads with unclear or conflicting replies and ask focused clarification questions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Revision Work
use_cases.description: Separate threads that need replies from settled decisions that should be handled by p11:revise.

prompt_templates.title: Review Current p11 Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:reply to review the current p11 document comments. Reply only where a visible thread needs clarification or input.

prompt_templates.title: Reply to a Specific Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:reply on this p11 read URL: [paste URL]. Review all visible threads and post concise replies where needed.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Conflicting Review Threads
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:reply to find visible threads with conflicting replies. Post a reply that names the decision needed to proceed.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Replies Before Posting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:reply to inspect the target comments, draft proposed replies, and ask me to approve them before posting.

output_examples.input: Review the comments on this p11 document and reply where needed.
output_examples.output: I found two visible threads that needed replies. I answered one clarification question and asked for a decision on the second thread.

output_examples.input: Check whether any unresolved p11 comments need a response.
output_examples.output: No visible thread needs a reply. Three comments contain clear revision requests that should be handled with the revision workflow.

output_examples.input: Use this edit URL and reply to comments that are blocking review.
output_examples.output: I recovered the matching read target, reviewed the visible comments, and posted concise replies to the blocking threads.

best_practices: Provide a p11 read URL or read ID when possible.
Review proposed replies before posting when the document is sensitive.
Use this skill for clarification replies, not for direct document revisions.
anti_patterns: Do not reply to resolved or hidden comments that reviewers will not see.
Do not treat every unresolved comment as needing a reply.
Do not install or upgrade the p11 CLI without user approval in controlled environments.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It reviews p11 document comment threads and posts replies when visible threads need clarification, input, or a decision.

faq.question: Does it edit the document?
faq.answer: No. It only replies to comments. Settled edit requests should be handled by a revision workflow.

faq.question: Does it need the p11 CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions use the p11 CLI and may ask to install or upgrade @p11-core/cli.

faq.question: Can it use an edit URL?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use context or p11 commands to recover the matching read target before replying.

faq.question: Should all unresolved comments get replies?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to reply only when the thread actually needs input, clarification, or acknowledgement.

faq.question: What security warning applies?
faq.answer: This skill runs external CLI commands and may post to p11 documents, so users should verify the target and approve command use.
